title: Quiz: Natural Gradient Descent
description: Test your understanding of natural gradient descent for variational quantum circuits

questions.question: What is the natural gradient update rule?
questions.explanation: The natural gradient update multiplies the gradient by the inverse Fisher information matrix: θ_{t+1} = θ_t - ηF⁻¹∇L, transforming to the steepest descent in probability space.

questions.question: Why is regularization needed when computing natural gradients?

options.text: To handle ill-conditioned or singular QFI matrices

questions.explanation: The QFI matrix can become ill-conditioned or near-singular, making direct inversion unstable. Regularization (like Tikhonov) adds λI to ensure numerical stability.

questions.question: What does parameterization invariance mean for natural gradient?

options.text: Optimization path is the same regardless of parameter coordinates

questions.explanation: Parameterization invariance means the optimization follows the same path in function space regardless of how parameters are defined, unlike standard gradient descent which depends on coordinate choice.

questions.question: What is Tikhonov regularization in the context of natural gradients?

options.text: Replacing F with F + λI before inversion
Adding λ to the loss function

questions.explanation: Tikhonov regularization adds λI to the Fisher matrix: F_reg = F + λI, ensuring invertibility and numerical stability while biasing toward standard gradient descent.

questions.question: Natural gradient descent typically converges faster than standard gradient descent for VQC optimization.
questions.explanation: True. Natural gradient accounts for parameter geometry, often converging in fewer iterations than standard gradient descent, especially on loss landscapes with varying curvature.

questions.question: What is the main computational overhead of natural gradient descent?
questions.explanation: Computing the QFI matrix requires O(p²) quantum circuits (p = number of parameters), and inversion is O(p³), making natural gradient computationally expensive.

questions.question: How does adaptive damping adjust the regularization parameter?

options.text: Increases λ if update is rejected, decreases if accepted
Sets λ equal to the learning rate

questions.explanation: Adaptive damping adjusts λ based on update quality: if an update reduces loss, λ is decreased (trusting curvature more); if loss increases, λ is increased (moving toward standard gradient).

questions.question: Which statements about natural gradient for VQCs are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.text: It requires computing the quantum Fisher information matrix
It always uses a constant learning rate
It provides steepest descent in KL divergence space
It can handle barren plateaus better than standard gradient

questions.explanation: Natural gradient requires QFI computation, provides steepest descent in KL divergence (probability) space, and can navigate barren plateaus better by rescaling gradients appropriately.
